plan: Trim SPY to 20% to cure concentration and reduce overall risk.
Add to QQQI, favoring income and broad index exposure.
Sell QQQ, SMH, SOXL, and SPAL to exit downtrends and remove all leveraged ETFs.
Trim CRWD to 10% to reduce single name concentration.
Buy OXY for diversification and its strong scanner read.
conservative.read: This mode targets ~1.5%/month, focusing on capital preservation. Expect lower drawdowns but also limited upside due to de-leveraging and diversification. Risk is significantly reduced, but market downturns can still impact returns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to cure concentration and manage overall risk.
Add to QQQI for balanced income and broad index exposure.
Sell QQQ, SMH, SOXL, and SPAL to exit downtrends and reduce leverage.
Add to CRWD, a quality tech name with positive P&L.
Buy TXN, a new quality tech name with a strong scanner setup.
balanced.read: This mode targets ~2.5%/month, balancing risk and growth. Leverage is reduced, and concentration is managed, aiming for steady growth with moderate drawdowns. Performance is tied to market conditions and quality tech sector.

moves.note: Exit below stop, no new add.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to cure concentration while maintaining exposure.
Sell QQQI and QQQ to exit income and downtrending broad index.
Add to CRWD, pressing a strong performer with positive P&L.
Add to SOXL, a leveraged ETF for aggressive semiconductor exposure.
Sell SPAL as it's below stop. Buy SOXS as a hedge for semiconductor exposure.
aggressive.read: This mode targets ~4%/month, aiming for aggressive growth with significantly higher risk. Expect larger drawdowns, especially given leveraged ETF exposure and sector concentration. Leveraged ETFs decay daily, magnifying both gains and losses; strict stop discipline is critical.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to cure concentration and manage risk.
Add significantly to QQQI, making it a core income holding.
Sell QQQ, SMH, SOXL, and SPAL to exit downtrends and remove all leveraged ETFs.
Trim CRWD to reduce high-beta single name exposure.
Buy OXY for diversification and its potential for steady yield.
income.read: This mode targets ~1%/month, prioritizing steady monthly distributions and capital preservation. While aiming for low drawdown, distributions are variable and NAV can still decline in market selloffs. This is not a savings account; market risk remains.
